nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Normandy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Normandy in northern France. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Normandin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Alain, Serge, Adrien, Andre, Aurore, Celine, Clovis, Emile, Gaetan, Gilles, Laurier.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  . Compare   Normandy  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

en.lifeSummary: Beatrix of Normandy was born in 1038, in Malton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 2 sons with Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick. She died in 1074, at the age of 36.
